name: Zhang Fei
description: Zhang Fei, also known as Yide, was a prominent general serving under the warlord Liu Bei during the late Eastern Han dynasty and the early Three Kingdoms period of China. Renowned for his unparalleled bravery, immense strength, and fiery temper, Zhang Fei was one of the Five Tiger Generals of Shu Han. Born in Zhuo Commandery, he joined Liu Bei and Guan Yu as sworn brothers, forming a formidable trio bound by loyalty and shared ambition. Throughout his military career, Zhang Fei participated in numerous battles, displaying exceptional martial prowess and often turning the tide of war with his ferocious charges and intimidating presence. His booming voice and imposing appearance were legendary, capable of striking fear into the hearts of his enemies. Zhang Fei's most famous feat was his stand at Changban Bridge, where he single-handedly deterred Cao Cao's pursuing army with his thunderous roar, allowing Liu Bei's forces and civilians to escape. Despite his valor, Zhang Fei's impulsive nature and excessive drinking often led to problems. He was eventually assassinated by his own subordinates, Fan Jiang and Zhang Da, while preparing for a campaign against the Kingdom of Wu. Zhang Fei remains a beloved and iconic figure in Chinese culture, celebrated for his courage, loyalty, and unwavering commitment to his sworn brothers. His legend continues to inspire awe and admiration, solidifying his place as one of the greatest warriors in Chinese history. He embodies the ideals of righteousness, bravery, and brotherhood, making him a timeless symbol of heroism.
subtitle: The Fierce General of Shu Han
achievements: Sworn brotherhood with Liu Bei and Guan Yu
Numerous victories in battles against Cao Cao
One of the Five Tiger Generals of Shu Han

keyMyths.title: The Oath of the Peach Garden
keyMyths.description: Zhang Fei, Liu Bei, and Guan Yu swore an oath of brotherhood in a peach garden, pledging loyalty and mutual support. This bond became the foundation of their shared ambition and shaped their destinies. The oath symbolizes unwavering loyalty and brotherhood.

keyMyths.title: Zhang Fei at Changban Bridge
keyMyths.description: Facing Cao Cao's pursuing army, Zhang Fei stood alone at Changban Bridge, roaring ferociously and deterring the enemy advance. His bravery allowed Liu Bei's forces and civilians to escape. This event highlights Zhang Fei's unparalleled courage and intimidation tactics.

keyMyths.title: Zhang Fei's Death
keyMyths.description: Due to his violent temper and drunken behavior, Zhang Fei was assassinated by his own subordinates, Fan Jiang and Zhang Da, while preparing for a campaign. This tragic end serves as a cautionary tale about the dangers of unchecked impulsiveness. The assassination was retribution for Zhang Fei's abuse.

sources.author: Luo Guanzhong
sources.description: The primary source for Zhang Fei's life and deeds, though often embellished with fictional elements.
sources.text: The primary source for Zhang Fei's life and deeds, though often embellished with fictional elements.

sources.author: Chen Shou
sources.description: A historical account providing factual information about Zhang Fei, though less detailed than the Romance.
